project.title: LC-MS by circHIPK3 probe method in human oral submucosal fibroblasts
project.projectDescription: To identify proteins to which circHIPK3 may bind and participate in the regulation of PDGF-BB-induced biological behavior in oral mucosal FBs, the circHIPK3 probe was designed for RNA pull-down experiment, and the proteins obtained from RNA pull-down were identified by silver staining, mass spectrometry and bioinformatics (COG, KEGG, PPI) analysis.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: ①According to the ID number of circHIPK3: hsa-circ-0000284, we searched all the base sequences in the circBase database.According to the sequence of circHIPK3, a biotinylated probe was designed and synthesized. ②Protein lysis: The cells in the 6-well plate were trypsinized and collected in an enzyme-free EP tube, and the IP lysate was added according to the ratio of cell precipitation:IP lysate = 1:6, and then lysed on ice for 30 min after shaking and mixing, shaking once every 10 min. After lysis, centrifuge at 12000 r/min at 4°C for 15 min, take the supernatant and put it in a new EP tube, and take 100 μL as input. ③Biotin-labeled circHIPK3 probe conjugated to streptavidin magnetic beads: Streptavidin magnetic beads were mixed upside down, and 50 μL was taken into the enzyme-free EP tubes of the NC group and the circHIPK3 probe group, respectively. The NC and probe sets were left on a magnet for 1 minute, the liquid was discarded, and the beads were resuspended with 500 μL of 1× RNA Capture Buffer after 2 washes with 1× Washing Buffer and 2 times with 20 mM Tris at pH 7.5;50 pmol Biotin-labeled NC/circHIPK3 probes were added, mixed well, and incubated at room temperature for 30-60 minutes. ④Protein binding to Biotin-labeled circHIPK3 probe: Place the NC and probe group tubes on a magnetic stand for 1 min, collect the beads, discard the liquid, wash 2 times with 20 mM pH 7.5 Tris, add 20 μL of 10× Protein-RNA Binding Buffer, 60 μL of 50% glycerol, 300 μL of protein solution at 2 mg/mL concentration, mix well, and incubate at 4°C on a silent mixer for 1-2 h. ⑤Elution of RNA-binding protein complexes: The NC and probe sets were placed on a magnetic stand for 1 minute, the beads were collected, and the supernatant was removed for recovery and preservation; Add 200 μL of 1× Washing Buffer and wash twice. Add 200 μL of 1× Washing Buffer again to mix the beads, of which 50 μL of mixture: Washing Buffer was washed 3 times, PBS washed 3 times, and stored at -20°C for subsequent mass spectrometry identification; 50 μL mixture: put the NC group and probe group tubes on the magnetic stand and let stand for 1 min, collect the magnetic beads, add 80 μL of eluent, boil water for 10 min, then put the NC group and probe group tubes on the magnetic stand and stand for 2 min, remove the supernatant, measure the egg white concentration and store at -80°C; Place 100 μL of the mixture on a magnet for 1 minute, collect the beads, and prepare for RNA purification. ⑥RNA purification: 500 μL of Trizol was added to the NC group and probe group tubes, mixed well, then 100 μL of chloroform and 10 μL of Proteinase K 18 μL were added, shaken vigorously for 30 s, and let stand for 5 min. ⑦Silver staining: The sample loading amount was calculated according to the protein concentration, the proteins of the NC group, the probe group, and the input group were added to the precast gel well tank, and the electrophoresis was stopped at 100 V until the purple bromophenol blue band in the sample reached the bottom of the gel. Peel off the precast gel in a glass dish, add deionized water on a shaker, and shake at room temperature for 5 min to decolorize. Discard the ionic water, add the fixative solution, and shake at room temperature for 30 min on a shaker for fixation. Discard the fixative solution, add deionized water and shake on the shaker for 5 min for cleaning, wash twice, add the allergenic solution, and shake it at room temperature for 30 min to sensitize it; Discard the sensitizing solution, add deionized water and shake on a shaker for 5 min for cleaning, wash twice, add staining solution, and shake at room temperature for 20 min on a shaker for staining. Discard the staining solution, add deionized water and shake on the shaker for 5 min for cleaning, wash twice, add the chromogenic solution, shake at room temperature on the shaker for 2 min, and take photos when clear bands appear. ⑧Mass spectrometry detection：The reaction solution (2.5% SDS/100 mM Tris-HCl, pH=8.5) was added to the samples and incubated at 95°C for 10 minutes. Centrifuge to remove the supernatant. The TCA precipitation method was used to precipitate the proteins in the upper fluid. The protein pellet was reconstituted with a complex solution (1% SDC/100 mM Tris-HCl, pH=8.5/10 mM TCEP/40 mM CAA) and incubated at 60 °C for 30 min for the reductive alkylation reaction. After diluting with an equal volume of ultrapure water, trypsin was added at a mass ratio of enzyme to protein at a mass ratio of 1:50, and incubated at 37 °C overnight with shaking for digestion. The next day, TFA was added to stop the digestion, and the supernatant was centrifuged at 12,000 g to make a homemade desalination column for desalting. After draining, prepare for testing.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: （1）Mass spectrometry data were retrieved using MaxQuant (V1.6.6) software using Andromeda database search algorithm. The database used for the search was the proteomic reference database of Human in Uniprot. The main search parameters were as follows: Oxidation (M), Acetyl (Protein N-term); Fixed modification Carbamidomethyl (C); Trypsin/P was digested by enzymatic digestion; The primary mass spectrometry matching tolerance was set to 20 ppm for the initial search and 4.5 ppm for the primary search; The secondary mass spectrometry matching tolerance is set to 20 ppm; Tick "Match Between Runs". The search results were screened based on 1% FDR at the protein and peptide levels, and the protein entries of the de-library protein, contaminating protein, and only one modified peptide were deleted, and the remaining identification information was used for subsequent analysis. （2）Feature annotations：By annotating and classifying the function, distribution, and pathways involved in proteins, it is convenient for data mining and finding rules, and provides reference and clues for subsequent research on biological and functional mechanisms. ①GO annotations:GO (Gene Ontology) is an internationally standardised classification system for gene function. GO can be divided into three categories, which describe the molecular function (MF), cellular component (CC), and biological process (BP) of a gene. In this project, we performed GO annotations for all identified proteins with three ontology classifications. ②KEGG Notes：KEGG (Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes) is a database that integrates genomic, chemical, and systemic functional information. Its KEGG pathway database is currently the most widely used and representative signaling pathway annotation database, including illustrated cell biochemical processes such as metabolism, membrane transport, signaling, cell cycle, and syngeneally conserved subpathways. In this project, we annotated the KEGG pathway for all identified proteins. ③COG annotations：COG (Clusters of Orthologous Groups of Proteins) is constructed according to the phylogenetic classification of the encoded proteins of bacteria, algae and eukaryotes. Each cluster of COGs is composed of homologous proteins, allowing inferences about the function of the sequence. In this project, we annotated the COG homology function of all identified proteins.
